items.title: Onboarding
items.name: onboarding
items.prompt: Audience-specific onboarding guides derived from the workspace layout, crate facades, and repository verification workflow. Sources: [Cargo.toml:1-76`, `README.md:1-8`, `crates/z00z_wallets/README.md:11-37`, `crates/z00z_simulator/README.md:6-30`, `.github/skills/z00z-full-verify-gate/scripts/full_verify.sh:64-103`.

children.title: Onboarding Hub
children.name: index
children.prompt: Landing page for contributor, staff, executive, and product-manager reading paths.

children.title: Contributor Guide
children.name: contributor-guide
children.prompt: Contributor path grounded in workspace manifests, verification gate, and stable crate facades.

children.title: Staff Engineer Guide
children.name: staff-engineer-guide
children.prompt: Architectural reading guide centered on ownership boundaries and stable facades.

children.title: Executive Guide
children.name: executive-guide
children.prompt: Capability, risk, and scaling overview sourced from crate boundaries and verification flows.

children.title: Product Manager Guide
children.name: product-manager-guide
children.prompt: User-facing capability and limitation guide translated from the codebase's object model and workflow boundaries.

items.title: Getting Started
items.name: 01-getting-started
items.prompt: Workspace-level setup, verification, and orientation docs derived from manifests and repository scripts. Sources: `Cargo.toml:1-76`, `README.md:1-8`, `deep-wiki-readme.md:29-70`, `.github/skills/z00z-full-verify-gate/scripts/full_verify.sh:64-103`.

children.title: Workspace Overview
children.name: workspace-overview
children.prompt: Explain why Z00Z is a Cargo workspace rather than one binary and map the major crate families.

children.title: Setup And Verify
children.name: setup-and-verify
children.prompt: Document reproducible setup and verification commands using the canonical repository gate and crate-specific commands.

children.title: Workspace Map
children.name: workspace-map
children.prompt: Catalogue the major crates and their responsibilities from crate roots and README boundaries.

children.title: Wiki Routing Status
children.name: wiki-routing-status
children.prompt: Explain why overview pages should remain landing or router pages, how deep leaf pages hang under them in the catalogue-driven wiki structure, and how to label repository surfaces as live, partial, adapter-only, placeholder, or planned.

items.title: Deep Dive
items.name: deep-dive
items.prompt: Architecture and subsystem documentation derived from crate roots, module facades, and owner READMEs.

children.title: Architecture
children.name: 02-architecture
children.prompt: System-level architecture and crate-boundary analysis.

children.title: System Overview
children.name: system-overview
children.prompt: Describe the system layers and primary runtime/data flow.

children.title: Crate Boundaries
children.name: crate-boundaries
children.prompt: Explain ownership seams and stable facades across the workspace.

children.title: Z00Z Utils Admission
children.name: z00z-utils-admission
children.prompt: Document z00z_utils as the cross-cutting admission-policy crate, its one-source-of-truth utility surfaces, and the boundary lines that keep domain semantics out.

children.title: Z00Z Crypto Facade
children.name: z00z-crypto-facade
children.prompt: Explain the stable z00z_crypto root facade, the expert lane, the explicit vendor Tari lane, and why workspace code should avoid direct Tari subpaths.

children.title: Extensions Boundary Rules
children.name: extensions-boundary-rules
children.prompt: Explain why z00z_extensions exists as a reserved extension namespace, which concerns must stay in owner crates or z00z_utils, and how roadmap examples fit the non-core add-on boundary without turning the crate into a dumping ground.

children.title: Core Protocol
children.name: 03-core-protocol
children.prompt: Core object model and genesis bootstrap authority.

children.title: Object Model And Genesis
children.name: object-model-and-genesis
children.prompt: Explain the genesis pipeline, object families, voucher bootstrap entries, and settlement artifacts.

children.title: Genesis Run Artifacts
children.name: genesis-run-artifacts
children.prompt: Walk run_genesis phase by phase and document the real output layout, export directories, and settlement manifest write path.

children.title: Genesis Manifest Refs
children.name: genesis-manifest-refs
children.prompt: Explain the canonical root manifest, manifest_refs rules, allowed sections, single-key subfiles, and unsupported actions_config handling.

children.title: Genesis Voucher Bootstrap
children.name: genesis-voucher-bootstrap
children.prompt: Clarify why vouchers enter genesis as VoucherBootstrapEntryV1 and how bootstrap records materialize into concrete voucher config with real policy and action identities.

children.title: Genesis Caveats
children.name: genesis-caveats
children.prompt: Document non-obvious genesis constraints: performance.num_threads is parsed, validated, and wired into a dedicated genesis Rayon pool in the inspected path, while genesis.rs now declares explicit internal submodules and genesis_output.rs remains the one canonical output entry with a nested support-module import.

children.title: Wallet And RPC
children.name: 04-wallet-and-rpc
children.prompt: Wallet surface, object inventory, and JSON-RPC wiring.

children.title: Wallet Architecture
children.name: wallet-architecture
children.prompt: Explain wallet ownership boundaries, object inventory, services, and RPC registration.

children.title: Wallet Stub Surface
children.name: wallet-stub-surface
children.prompt: Map which wallet surfaces are genuinely stateful today, which are mixed, and which are still placeholders.

children.title: Wallet RPC Gaps
children.name: wallet-rpc-gaps
children.prompt: Document the real wallet RPC wiring state, current missing routes, and where static audit output can overstate gaps.

children.title: Storage Runtime And Rollup
children.name: 05-storage-runtime
children.prompt: Settlement storage, runtime planners, validators, watchers, and rollup verification.

children.title: Settlement Runtime And Rollup
children.name: settlement-runtime-and-rollup
children.prompt: Explain settlement storage ownership and how runtime and rollup layers compose it.

children.title: Object Package Rejects
children.name: object-package-rejects
children.prompt: Explain the typed object admission package, reject-code taxonomy, and wallet or validator integration path.

children.title: Publication Route Authority
children.name: publication-route-authority
children.prompt: Explain why runtime owns publication binding construction, storage owns route verification, and downstream crates must not fork either path.

children.title: Settlement Path Proofs
children.name: settlement-path-proofs
children.prompt: Explain the canonical definition_id to serial_id to terminal_id path, semantic versus physical roots, and storage-owned proof boundaries.

children.title: Settlement Recovery Failover
children.name: settlement-recovery-failover
children.prompt: Document SettlementRecoveryState, journal lineage, same-lineage takeover rules, and fail-closed restart behavior across storage and runtime.

children.title: Runtime Aggregator Surface
children.name: runtime-aggregator-surface
children.prompt: Expand the runtime aggregator layer into planner, placement, dispatch, simulation, and recovery invariants instead of leaving them inside one overview chapter.

children.title: Rollup Theorem Verifier
children.name: rollup-theorem-verifier
children.prompt: Explain exactly what the rollup theorem verifier checks, what it intentionally does not reconstruct, and how it differs from broader preflight gates.

children.title: Validator Verdict Lanes
children.name: validator-verdict-lanes
children.prompt: Document the validator-owned verdict surface, how checkpoint and typed object verdicts compose, and how object reject codes collapse into stable reject classes.

children.title: Watchers Alert Boundaries
children.name: watchers-alert-boundaries
children.prompt: Document the watcher-owned observation surface, alert families, evidence export, and the non-authoritative boundary that keeps publication, route acceptance, and settlement truth with runtime, validators, and storage.

children.title: Simulator And Quality
children.name: 06-simulator-and-quality
children.prompt: Scenario harness, release-packet boundaries, and verification flow.

children.title: Scenario Pipeline
children.name: scenario-pipeline
children.prompt: Document scenario_1 as the canonical integration harness and explain how it interacts with the release-style verify gate.

children.title: Scenario1 Object Artifacts
children.name: scenario1-object-artifacts
children.prompt: Map scenario_1 object flows, emitted public artifacts, Stage 13 evidence, and the strict packet contract enforced by the simulator tests.

children.title: Scenario1 Runner Guards
children.name: scenario1-runner-guards
children.prompt: Explain the canonical 13-stage scenario_1 runner contract, sandbox and process guards, and the drift checks that keep the emitted release packet aligned with the design and trace contracts.

children.title: Networking And Observability
children.name: 07-networking-and-observability
children.prompt: RPC transport, OnionNet placeholder boundary, and telemetry/watcher surfaces.

children.title: Networking And Telemetry
children.name: networking-and-telemetry
children.prompt: Describe RPC, privacy-overlay placeholder seams, and workspace observability boundaries.

children.title: OnionNet Target Architecture
children.name: onionnet-target-architecture
children.prompt: Separate the current OnionNet placeholder crate from the intended deterministic anonymous-ingress architecture and its unfrozen blockers.

children.title: RPC Overlay Boundary
children.name: rpc-overlay-boundary
children.prompt: Separate the transport-focused RPC crate from the future OnionNet privacy overlay so request transport and anonymous ingress do not read as interchangeable layers.
